subject: Sports
title: Wingsuit Flying: Closest Humans Get to Flying
description: A micro-lesson that makes Wingsuit Flying: Closest Humans Get to Flying usable.

hook.text: They jump off cliffs in fabric suits and glide at 120 mph, feet from rock faces. Wingsuit flying is insanely dangerous.

buildup.text: Wingsuit flying uses a suit with fabric between the arms and legs that creates an airfoil shape for gliding.

discovery.text: Modern wingsuits achieve a 3:1 glide ratio—for every meter dropped, the pilot travels three meters forward.

twist.text: Proximity flying—skimming terrain at high speed—has killed dozens of athletes. The fatality rate is staggering.

climax.text: Despite the danger, the sport grows yearly. The sensation of human flight is apparently worth the risk.

punchline.text: Three meters forward for every one down. The closest to flying humans will get.

quiz.question: What glide ratio can modern wingsuits achieve?
options: 3:1—three meters forward for every meter dropped
